spec_match.rationale: Uses `rec.mediaStreamTrack = track` then `rec.start()`, demonstrating that a track-bound recognizer reads from the same source as a parallel Web Audio analyser — the dual-pipeline claim the page makes.

interactive.rationale: Live VU meter and live transcript driven by real `getUserMedia`, `AudioContext`, and `SpeechRecognition`. Start/stop both work, RAF-driven peak meter updates at ~60fps.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises a mic track feeding SR while a parallel Web Audio analyser draws a meter from the same track — exactly what's wired.

edge_cases.rationale: Detects missing `mediaStreamTrack` property and falls back gracefully, but doesn't exercise tab-capture or virtual-audio-device scenarios that the lede mentions, and doesn't show the recognizer cleanup when the track ends.

references.rationale: Links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5178378591698944 — note this ID (...591698944) differs from the feature index ID (...197139456). One of them is wrong; needs reconciling against the milestone listing.

design_system.rationale: All CSS variables; warn-block uses accent-rose semantic colour; no static cards; mobile-responsive grid.

openQuestions.title: Fix the ChromeStatus reference ID mismatch
openQuestions.detail: This page links chromestatus feature 5178378591698944. The feature index links 5178378197139456. The cleanup script at /tmp/fix-slugs.py uses the embedded ID to recover slug names, so a wrong ID here would mis-slug the folder. Verify which ID is canonical (the listing name from milestone=135 is the source of truth) and update.

openQuestions.title: Reconcile API shape across sibling concepts
openQuestions.detail: This page uses `rec.mediaStreamTrack = track`. captioned-meeting uses `rec.start(track)`. multi-source-captions uses `rec.audioTrack = track`. Three concepts, three different property names — at most one is correct. Read the spec PR and the Chrome 135 ship and normalise.

openQuestions.title: Demo the headline alternate sources
openQuestions.detail: Lede mentions virtual audio devices, tab capture, and processed Web Audio output as use cases the new track support enables. None of these are exercised — only `getUserMedia({audio:true})` runs. Add a `feed processed audio` button that pipes the mic through a biquad filter then into both the analyser and the recognizer, to actually demonstrate the `processed Web Audio output` claim.

openQuestions.title: Handle track.onended
openQuestions.detail: When the user unplugs the mic or closes the tab capture, what happens to the recognizer? Add an `onended` listener that gracefully stops the recognizer and surfaces a `track ended` note in the UI.

summary: Clean dual-pipeline demo with a real audio meter and a real recognizer, but the chromestatus reference ID is wrong (mismatches the feature index) and the API surface uses a different property name than its sibling concepts. Major spec-alignment work needed across the feature.
